hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware DiT+EGNN diffusion model is trained on AIMNet2-relaxed ChEMBL3D conformers with explicit chirality/E–Z graph augmentation, then it will efficiently generate diverse, topology- and stereochemistry-preserving molecular conformers with near-DFT energies that outperform classical conformer generators across drug-like (including macrocyclic) molecules, because the equivariant architecture and stereochemical constraints guide sampling toward physically realistic low-energy minima.
